xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: No.2 INDIA EXCHANGE PLACE, 3RD FLOOR, KOLKATA, 700001
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesGeneralDisclosureExplanat:
UCO Bank is not registered under Companies Act. Hence Bank does not have CIN for the purpose of validation of file.We have filed Dummy CIN.
Point 23- No. of International offices includes representative office of UCO bank at Tehran, Iran. No. of National offices include 3412 domestic branches, 2647 ATM centres, 11136 Business Correspondent units and 50 Administrative offices of the Bank.
Point 26- Key Managerial Personnel includes 3 whole time directors, Chief Financial Officer and Company secretary.
Point 28- Bank has no holding / subsidiary / associate companies / joint ventures as on 31.03.2026.
Point 29- Amount in crore
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheCompanyOrLLPOrFirmOfA: M/S K GHOSH & ASSOCIATES, CHARTERED ACCOUNTANT
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksForAssuranceOfDetailsOf: Only for Point C- Spending on measures towards well being of employees.
Only for Point b- Gross wages paid to female as a percentage of total wages.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: THE BUSINESS OPERATIONS OF THE BANK ARE BROADLY CLASSIFIED INTO FOUR SEGMENTS: A. CORPORATE/WHOLESALE BANKING B. RETAIL BANKING C. TREASURY D. OTHER BANKING SERVICES
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Bank provides wide array of product and services and caters to the diverse customer base including individuals, farmers, MSME’s, Corporates, Government entities, Self Help Groups, Trusts, Clubs etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsForGrievanceRedressalMe: BEING A FINANCIAL SERVICE PROVIDER, BANK HAS GRIEVANCE REDRESSAL MECHANISM FOR ITS CUSTOMERS, INVESTORS, SHAREHOLDERS, AND EMPLOYEES.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkForGrievanceRedressPoli: Bank has separate investor cell at Head office
Bank has separate investor cell at Head office
Redressal through Bank's Internal HRMS Portal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RemarksStakeHolderGroupFromWho: Out of 3770 pending complaints, 1057 are general grievances, 2692 are failed/disputed transaction related grievances and 21 complaints related to Banking Ombudsman.
Out of 2128 pending complaints, 1094 are general grievances, 990 are failed/disputed transaction related grievances and 44 complaints related to Banking Ombudsman.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Strong governance is essential to maintain stakeholder trust, prevent fraud, and ensure compliance with regulatory expectations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Adoption of Board-approved Code of Conduct, regular ethics training, anonymous whistleblower channels, internal vigilance mechanisms.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Financial inclusion is key to expanding the customer base, fulfilling regulatory mandates, and strengthening the bank’s social impact.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Targeted outreach in rural/semi-urban areas, BC (Business Correspondent) model, financial literacy programs, and digital banking initiatives.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Increasing digitization brings risk of cyberattacks and data breaches, which can harm reputation and invite legal liabilities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementation of robust IT security systems (firewalls, DLP tools), periodic cyber audits, CERT-In compliance, staff sensitization programs.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Misleading product information or unresolved complaints can lead to regulatory scrutiny, reputational damage, and customer attrition.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Standardized product disclosures, multi-channel grievance mechanisms (digital, branch, call center), customer service audits, and complaint tracking systems.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Transparency in Dealings with Borrowers and MSMEs
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: MSMEs often face difficulty in understanding terms and conditions, leading to disputes, stress accounts, and credit discipline issues.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Simplified documentation, transparency in interest/pricing, restructuring support under RBI frameworks, and grievance redressal for borrowers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Compliance with AML and KYC Regulations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Non-compliance with AML/KYC norms can lead to severe regulatory penalties and increased financial crime exposure.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Centralized KYC validation, risk-based customer due diligence, transaction monitoring tools, and regular staff training in line with PMLA guidelines.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: A diverse and skilled board enhances strategic oversight, risk management, and long-term sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Appointment of independent directors with expertise in banking, law, technology, gender inclusion, regular board evaluations and skill matrix assessments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Staff conduct directly impacts customer trust and operational integrity; lack of training may lead to regulatory or operational errors.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Mandatory annual ethics and compliance training, refresher courses on customer service, digital literacy, vigilance awareness, and HR disciplinary procedures.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Increasing reliance on third-party vendors for IT, customer service, and operations exposes the Bank to reputational and operational risks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementation of vendor due diligence, SLAs with performance and compliance clauses, regular audits, and business continuity plans for outsourced services.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Climate Risk and Environmental Impact of Operations
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Stakeholders and regulators expect banks to address their environmental footprint and support green financing.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Adoption of green practices (energy-efficient branches, paperless banking), promotion of ESG-linked loans, and carbon footprint disclosure initiatives.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MaterialIssueIdentified: Interest Rate Risk in Banking Book
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Regulator's decision of modification in interest rates impacts Bank's interest margins which in turn effects the profitability of the Bank.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Bank is employing various techniques to address interest risk in Banking Book including hedging & pricing of its deposit products accordingly.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Bank needs to carry enough liquidity to address any unforeseen circumstances leading to a liquidity run on the bank.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Bank is actively managing its liquidity position by regularly monitoring its stock/flow ratios and other regulatory metrics such as LCR and NSFR.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheNationalAndInternatio: Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).

Bank has obtained ISO 27001:2013 for Information Security Management System. The scope of activities includes implementation of information security, Cyber Security & Privacy Protection, Data Centres(DC) and Disaster Recovery Sites (DRS).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.

The Bank has adopted a Net Zero Transition Plan aligned with Government of India climate commitments and NDCs, and follows PCAF standards for financed emissions assessment. • Net Zero Target: By 2056 (operations and financed portfolio) • Scope 1 & 2: Net zero by 2050 • Scope 3: Emission intensity stabilisation during 2026–2028 (base year: 2025), followed by gradual reduction to achieve net zero by 2056 • Green Financing: Progressive increase in green lending to support portfolio decarbonisation The approach is aligned with sectoral pathways and evolving regulatory framework.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: The Bank places significant emphasis on integrating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) principles into its core policies. With a focus on transparency, ethical practices, and innovation, the Bank has launched customer-centric products, adopted cutting-edge digital solutions, and prioritized employee welfare. Sustainable practices, such as reducing electricity, fuel, and paper consumption, and strengthening cyber and information security, reflect our commitment to ESG. The Bank's corporate governance is grounded in the principles of professionalism and transparency, and it continues to lead efforts in financial inclusion, bringing marginalized communities into the formal banking system. This has helped foster a culture of savings among those previously excluded from the economic mainstream. Recognizing climate change as both an environmental and business risk, the Bank is proactive in mitigating its carbon footprint through sustainable practices. These include encouraging paperless transactions, using eco-friendly refrigerants, green generators, and energy-efficient lighting across branches. Regular discussions with stakeholders on ESG practices ensure continuous innovation in products and services, reinforcing our long-term sustainability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheHighestAuthorityRe: Board of Directors of the Bank
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfSpecifiedCommitteeOfT: All committees of the Board look after the sustainability related issues as per their agenda specific requirement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesManagementAndProcessDiscl: Note to point 10- BR Report of the Bank is reviewed by Board of Directors on annual basis. During this assessment, the efficacies of the policies are reviewed and necessary changes, if any, to policies and procedures are implemented. The Bank is in compliance with the extant regulations as applicable.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:TopicsOrPrinciplesCoveredUnder: Areas relating to Credit Risk Management, etc.
Leadership Development, Operational Risk Management, Cyber Security Awareness, Compliance
Ethics, Governance, Induction, Orientation, Skill Development program
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:BriefOfTheMonetaryCaseForPenal: Penalty/Fine/Settlement/Compounding fee, if any, paid by the Bank is not material as per the Materiality policy of the Bank. Details of penalty are being disclosed on a quarterly basis in the notes to accounts forming part of financial results.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: The Bank has framed Whistle Blower Policy and the same is available on Bank’s Website.(Weblink https://uco.bank.in/documents/d/guest/whistle-blower-policy ) The objective of the policy is to reduce corruption and safeguarding whistle blower who sounded the misuse of power by Executives/Officers in the bank from victimization. The Bank is committed towards conducting the business and dealing with all its stakeholders, with highest ethical standards and in compliance with all the applicable laws and regulations. A separate link for the Whistle Blower has been created inside Bank’s Internal portal. The link is accessible to all employees of the Bank to share information about any unlawful activities going on within organization / frauds / steps required to implement preventive vigilance. The portal will capture all information about the Whistle Blower and complete details of incident/information. The details entered using this link portal will be accessed exclusively by Chairman of the Audit Committee of Board.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: The Bank has established processes to avoid and manage conflicts of interest involving its Board members. As part of this process, Bank obtains declaration on an annual basis from each Director disclosing their interests/associations with other entities. Under its Corporate Governance framework, the Bank has implemented a Code of Conduct applicable to all Directors and Core Management personnel. This Code of Conduct outlines the dos and don’ts to prevent conflicts of interest and restrains Directors from engaging in any activities that may give rise to such conflicts. Each Director affirms compliance with the Code of Conduct through a signed declaration on an annual basis.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple1ExplanatoryText:
Point 2- Monetary Penalty/Fine/Settlement/Compounding fee, if any, paid by the Bank is not material as per the Materiality policy of the Bank. Details of penalty are being disclosed on a quarterly basis in the notes to accounts forming part of financial results.
Point no. 8- Account Payable amount is taken in crore which is multiplied by 365. Cost of goods/services procured is taken in crore.
Point no. 9- AS per RBI guidelines, Related parties for a bank are its parent, subsidiay(ies), associates/joint ventures, Key Management Personnel (KMP) and relatives of KMP. Bank doesnot have subsidiary or joint venture as on 31.03.2026. Bank had one Associate namely Paschim Banaga Gramin Bank which got amalgamated w.e.f 01.05.2025.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: Given the nature of business, Bank has limited scope to use recycled material as processed inputs. However, Bank has IT Asset Disposal policy in terms of which IT assets of the Bank cannot be reused once it reaches end of support or becomes obsolete and are disposed off as scrap to approved e-waste dealers/recyclers once they become irreparable or non-reusable.
Owing to the nature of business, there is no generation of hazardous waste.
Not relevant owing to the nature of business. However, Bank reduced the use of paper in its operations by going digital.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple2ExplanatoryText: Point 1- Given the nature of bank there were limited opportunities and scope for R&D investments in specific technologies that could enhance the environmental and social impacts of banking products and processes. Bank capital investment is largely restricted to Information Technology (IT) capex. Greater adoption of digital platforms not only brings in increased efficiencies of operations but also ensures substantially reduced consumption of paper, thereby indirectly reducing the carbon footprints. Ewaste is given in tonnes.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Bank has a Grievance Redressal Mechanism for its employees. Employees can raise their grievances through Bank Internal HRMS Portal.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: We have an active occupational health and safety management system at all the branches & administrative offices spreading across Pan India.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: Given the nature of business, this is not directly applicable. However, fire mock drills are conducted on a periodical basis for assessing the risks and creating awareness among the employees concerned.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProcessesForWorkersToReportThe: Bank has no workers as defined in the guidance note of BRSR.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: In adherence to our commitment to safety and security, we have implemented comprehensive measures across all branches and administrative offices. This includes the installation of fire extinguishers to mitigate fire risks and the deployment of fire hydrants and alarm systems in our high-rise buildings. Furthermore, to safeguard both our assets and the well-being of our personnel, we have deployed CCTV surveillance systems and Digital Fire cum Burglar alarm systems throughout our branches nationwide. At our Head Offices, we have integrated Active Visitor Management system and Flap barriers to regulate visitor access effectively. Additionally, X-ray baggage scanner machines have been installed to thoroughly screen the belongings of both staff and visitors, ensuring the identification and prevention of potential threats or prohibited items.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Bank organized a mock fire drill in collaboration with the Police Department and Fire & Emergency Services Department of West Bengal at our Head Office from time to time to enhance awareness and equip our personnel with the necessary skills to respond effectively to unforeseen fire incidents. Furthermore, necessary instructions and comprehensive guidelines for fire safety are prominently displayed at all our branches and administrative offices for the appropriate usage of fire extinguishers. Staff members were also provided with an awareness session on the correct handling and effective use of portable fire extinguishers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: The Bank ensures that statutory dues as applicable to the transactions within the remit of the Bank are deducted and deposited in accordance with extant regulations. This activity is also reviewed as part of the internal and statutory audit. The Bank expects its value chain partners to uphold business responsibility principles and values of transparency and accountability.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: Being a banking entity, stakeholders of the bank are, investors, vendors, Government, Regulators, employees, customers, communities at large etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: Several measures undertaken by the Bank under economic, environmental, and social segments are presented in the Directors Report and Management Discussion and Analysis Section of the Annual Report, which is shared with shareholders and published on the Bank’s website. Shareholders are provided opportunities to deliberate with members of the Board on various topics, including economic, environmental, and social aspects, during shareholder meetings. The Bank also conducts customer meets and town hall meetings at regular intervals, where issues related to these segments are discussed and addressed.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Yes. Inputs received from stakeholders are taken into account while formulating policies and designing products. Feedback from customer meets, town hall sessions, and grievance channels has helped the Bank enhance service delivery, promote inclusive banking, and incorporate environmentally and socially responsible practices into its offerings.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: The Bank actively engages with and supports vulnerable and marginalized stakeholder groups by aligning with various Government initiatives aimed at financial inclusion and social upliftment. Through schemes such as PMAY (home loans), MUDRA loans, and PM SVANidhi, the Bank provides accessible credit to economically weaker sections, low-income groups, small and marginal farmers, women beneficiaries, and other underserved communities. These efforts reflect the Bank’s continued commitment to empowering vulnerable segments and promoting inclusive growth.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: SMS, E-mail and Direct and other modes of communication
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: To Keep them updated about the mission and new policies of the Bank
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOtherChannelsOfCommun: Newspaper, Website of Bank and stock Exchanges, Shareholder’s Meeting, Email
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PurposeAndScopeOfEngagementInc: To Keep them updated about the latest development of the Bank
To Keep them updated about the latest development of the Bank
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: Bank ensure to maintain healthy and safe working environment for our employees irrespective of their Gender, Caste, Ethnicity, Designation, etc. Also Bank has Policy in place for Prevention of Sexual Harassment of Women Employees, Equal Opportunity Policy etc. Employees can also raise their issue through Grievance Redressal Mechanism provided in Bank’s intranet HRMS portal for resolution of their Grievances.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: Bank has constituted Internal Complaints Committee at both Zonal Level and Apex Level at Head Office under the provisions of POSH Act, 2013. Bank also has Grievance Redressal Officers appointed both at Zonal and Head Office levels for resolution of Grievances of Specially abled employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfABusinessProcessBeing: Bank has formulated the Policy on Prevention of Sexual Harassment at Workplace and Equal Opportunity Policy. Bank has also sensitized all employees for observing and protection of Human Rights in all the walks of their life.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: The Bank endeavors to maintain a safe and conducive working environment providing equal opportunities for all employee of the Bank. Issues related to discrimination based on gender, nationality, religion, caste, disability, language, etc. are investigated with proper scrutiny as per the provisions of the existing polices, laws and regulations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: Reasonable Assurance carried out by K Ghosh & Associates, Chartered Accountants.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyInCaseA: Yes, Reasonable Assurance carried out by K Ghosh & Associates, Chartered Accountants.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyThatUnd: Reasonable Assurance carried out by K Ghosh & Associates, Chartered Accountants. Note : 1) Emission factor for electricity used for FY 2025-26 and 2024-25 is 0.727 (derived from the Central Electricity Authority (CEA)’s CO2 Baseline Database for the Indian Power Sector, Version 20.0) 2) Emission Factor for Petrol used for FY 2025-26 and 2024-25 is 2.27 (As per India GHG
Yes, Reasonable Assurance carried out by K Ghosh & Associates, Chartered Accountants.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Given the nature of the business, no hazardous and toxic chemicals used in operations. For waste management practices, refer Principle 2 (Essential Indicator-Point 3)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi:
Bank has a Board approved Business Continuity Policy (BCP) which primarily serves as insurance against disasters. BCP includes standards and procedures to ensure continuity, resumption and recovery of critical business processes, at an agreed level and limit the impact of the disaster on people, processes and infrastructure or to minimize the operational, financial, legal, reputational and other material consequences arising from such a disaster.
There are various threats and vulnerabilities to which banking entities are exposed. There could be natural disasters, intentional disasters, IT related disasters, disaster due to human errors resulting in disruption of business.
The Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) for IT Systems shall be an integral part of the BCP, covering the strategic and operational framework of the people, processes and infrastructure in the event of disasters in IT environment
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple7ExplanatoryText: The Bank does not engage in policy advocacy, but is actively involved in consultation/ discussion forums with the Government and other bodies in the banking industry.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheTradeAndIndustryChamb: Foreign Exchange Dealers Association of India (FEDAI)
Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association (FIMMDA)
Indian Institute of Banking and Finance (IIBF)
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: Bank’s operations do not adversely impact nearby communities. However, for any grievance redressal, customers/communities are given multiple options to raise the matter against the Bank’s service, products and to provide their feedback / suggestions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple8ExplanatoryText: Point 6- Number of people benefitted from CSR activities cannot be ascertained. Hence we have provided a tentative in order to verify the excel sheet, since it is a mandatory field.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:CSRProject:
1. Livelihood Enhancement & Skill Development • Supported nutritional arrangements for backward/ tribal youth enrolled in a Football Academy run by Siliguri Municipal Corporation. • Continued partnership with Poornatha & Co. for conducting training programmes for MSME customers. • Provided computer infrastructure to educational institutions such as Chhatrapati Maharaj College (Kolhapur) and Tihu College (Assam).
2. Health & Sanitation • Supported installation of dialysis machines at JITO Day Care Centre, Kolkata. • Provided ambulances/emergency vehicles to medical institutions including GC Medical College (Assam), Dubri Medical College, and Aizawl Municipal Corporation. • Sponsored health-related initiatives including participation in health and environment fairs. 3. Education & Capacity Building • Contributed towards development of library facilities at Babu Jagjivan Ram Chhatravas (Siliguri). • Supported infrastructure development at institutions such as Assam University and IIIT Bhagalpur (including green mobility initiatives like electric golf carts). 4. Environmental Sustainability & Sanitation • Installed rainwater harvesting systems at Yogada Satsang Mahavidyalaya, Ranchi. • Provided equipment such as leaf shredders to Municipal Corporations (Jalandhar) to support waste management. • Promoted green mobility through electric passenger vehicles for institutions like Fakirmohan University (Odisha).
5. Drinking Water & Public Utilities • Installed water ATMs in Varanasi and drinking water facilities at multiple locations including Head Office, Kolkata. • Supported water purification systems in rural areas such as Rohtak district. • Contributed to drinking water arrangements for Amarnath Yatra pilgrims. 6. Infrastructure Development & Public Welfare • Supported construction of sanitation facilities (toilets and drinking water) at religious and public places including Giriraj Ji Maharaj Temple (Meerut). • Provided air conditioning systems, electrical equipment, and public utility infrastructure to government offices and temples across locations. • Contributed to development and beautification of public parks and civic infrastructure. 7. Community Welfare & Relief • Provided CSR support for disaster-affected families through District Administration, Uttarakhand. • Supported welfare initiatives such as ration distribution, meal services, and blanket distribution for vulnerable groups including visually impaired institutions in Kolkata. • Extended support to sanitation workers through provision of winter uniforms (Aligarh).
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:FromWhichMarginalizedOrVulnera: The Bank does not have a preferential procurement process. However, it encourages procurement from MSME and marginalized/vulnerable groups.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: The Bank has established comprehensive mechanisms to efficiently handle consumer complaints and feedback: Complaint Channels: In Person: Customers can submit complaints directly at the branch, zonal office, or head office, and receive an acknowledgment. Complaints can also be dropped in the complaint box. Phone: Grievances can be lodged via the bank's toll-free helpline (1800 8910) available 24/7. Email/Website: Complaints can be sent to hosp.cscell@uco.bank.in or through the online grievance system at https://spgrs.ucoonline.in, accessible 24/7. Acknowledgment: Written complaints are acknowledged within three days, while complaints lodged via phone or the online system are provided with a reference or acknowledgment number. The Bank has established a centralized mechanism for capturing and monitoring customer grievances. All complaints received through branches, zonal offices, Head Office, as well as those received from regulatory and government authorities—including Central/State Governments, Ministries, Government Departments, Reserve Bank of India, Members of Parliament/Legislative Councils, IBA, and other agencies—through physical or electronic modes are recorded in the Online Grievance Redressal System (OGRS) to ensure effective tracking and resolution. Customer Feedback Mechanism: The Bank actively engages with customers to obtain feedback for continuous improvement in service delivery. Feedback is collected through multiple channels, including the Bank’s website, QR codes displayed at branches, ATMs, mobile banking, and e-banking platforms. In addition, an employee-specific feedback mechanism is in place wherein customers visiting branches are encouraged to share their experience regarding service quality and staff responsiveness at transaction points. Further, Bank has separate cell for redressing the grievances of the shareholder queries/complaints at Head Office. An exclusive email id for shareholders queries/complaint is hosgr.calcutta@uco.bank.in
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfThePolicyOnCyberSecur: Bank has board approved Cyber Security Policy and Data Security & Privacy Policy and the aforesaid policies are available at Bank's Internal UCO Online Portal
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionsT: To address cyber risks arising from human vulnerabilities, the Bank continues to undertake multiple preventive and corrective measures to enhance customer awareness on emerging cyber threats and associated safety practices. • Graphic and illustrative cyber security advisories are disseminated through the Bank’s official social media platforms. • Periodic push notifications and customized emails are sent to customers highlighting cyber risks and preventive measures. • Awareness seminars, webinars, and training programmes are conducted regularly to strengthen employees’ cyber security preparedness. • Cyber security advisories are displayed at branches and ATM premises for wider customer outreach. • Security alerts and best practices are integrated across digital channels including ATM screens, Internet Banking, and Mobile Banking platforms. • A dedicated ‘Cyber Security Awareness Corner’ is maintained on the Bank’s website to promote cyber safety among customers and the general public. Through these ongoing initiatives, the Bank continues to strengthen customer protection and enhance its overall cyber resilience framework.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WeblinkWhereInformationOnProdu:
Information about products/services offered can be accessed through Bank’s Website (www.uco.bank.in). List of Social Media Channels where bank disseminates information on its products and services :
Facebook https://www.facebook.com/official.ucobank/ X https://twitter.com/UCOBankOfficial LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/3205517/ Instagram https://www.instagram.com/official.ucobank/ YouTube https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCuXnmG5210XSLSwyyJsiYQ/featured Threads https://www.threads.com/@official.ucobank
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StepsTakenToInformAndEducateCo: Bank is regularly sending cyber education/awareness messages to the customers on Digital Products and their safe usage through SMS, E-Mail, Push notifications as well as through Bank’s Social Media handles.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsInPlaceToInformConsu:
Bank has following mechanism in place to inform consumers of any risk of disruption/discontinuation of essential services : a) Prior to any scheduled maintenance activities affecting vital systems such as M-Banking, E-Banking, and ATMs, bank ensures customers are informed in advance through direct communication channels like SMS and push notifications, allowing customers to plan accordingly and mitigate any inconvenience.
b) Bank displays informative messages on multiple platforms including the bank’s website, e-banking portal and ATM screens, ensuring customers are kept well-informed about any upcoming service interruptions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesPrinciple9ExplanatoryText: Yes, Bank carried out various surveys among customers on their satisfaction relating to major products/services through feedback system, made available on major channels like M-Banking, E-Banking, Kiosk etc. Further, Bank also sends feedback link to customers on any transaction for their valuable feedback/rating/suggestion. Bank displays product information as per local laws.
